channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.123
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.123 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: New MAI models in Microsoft Foundry across text, image, voice, and speech
item.description: Continuing Microsoft AI momentum in Foundry Since launching MAI-Image-2-Efficient, MAI-Image-2, MAI-Voice-1, MAI-Transcribe-1 in Microsoft Foundry this spring, we've been laser-focused on one thing: giving developers the most complete first-party AI stack to build with. Today, at Microsoft Build...
item.dc:creator: Naomi Moneypenny

item.title: Make any agent enterprise-ready with the Agent 365 SDK
item.description: One of the biggest barriers to enterprise adoption is the lack of centralized controls. Before deploying an agent broadly, organizations need clear answers: What is this agent allowed to do? What data can it access? How is it monitored? And how do we step in when something goes wrong? Today,...
item.dc:creator: aaronbjork

item.title: VNet integration for Azure SRE Agent (preview)
item.description: For many production systems, the logs, databases, private endpoints, repositories, and runbooks an SRE Agent needs to do its job are behind network boundaries your security team already governs. VNet integration for Azure SRE Agent, now in preview, puts the agent's outbound traffic under those same...
item.dc:creator: sanchitmehta

item.title: Expanded technical preview availability for the GitHub Copilot app
item.description: The GitHub Copilot app technical preview is now available to all existing Copilot Pro, Pro+, Business, and Enterprise customers. Download the Copilot app for Windows, macOS, or Linux to get… The post Expanded technical preview availability for the GitHub Copilot app appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Copilot app: The agent-native desktop experience
item.description: At Microsoft Build 2026, GitHub introduced new tools, updates, and surfaces so agents can work the way you already work. The post GitHub Copilot app: The agent-native desktop experience appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Copilot SDK is now generally available
item.description: The GitHub Copilot SDK is now generally available. You can embed GitHub Copilot’s agentic engine into your own applications, services, and developer tools with a stable API and production-ready support.… The post Copilot SDK is now generally available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Managed Connectors for SRE Agent (preview)- Govern what your agent can do
item.description: Giving an agent access to a tool is the easy part. The harder question is what it's allowed to do with that access. "Can the agent copy a file in OneDrive?" mostly answers itself. "Can it copy any file, to any destination, over one that's already there?" is the one that decides whether the...
item.dc:creator: Dalibor_Kovacevic

item.title: Copilot CLI: Improved UI, rubber duck, prompt scheduling, and voice input
item.description: GitHub Copilot CLI is getting a major refresh at Microsoft Build 2026. Rubber duck, prompt scheduling, and voice input are generally available today, and a new experimental terminal interface—including tabs… The post Copilot CLI: Improved UI, rubber duck, prompt scheduling, and voice input appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Cloud and local sandboxes for GitHub Copilot now in public preview
item.description: GitHub Copilot can now run inside secure, isolated sandboxes, both locally on your machine and in the cloud. Sandboxed Copilot experiences provide isolated environments for Copilot’s tool execution locally as… The post Cloud and local sandboxes for GitHub Copilot now in public preview appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Copilot code review for Azure Repos is now in technical preview
item.description: GitHub Copilot code review for Azure Repos is now available in technical preview, bringing on demand pull request reviews directly into your Azure DevOps workflow. How it works Once enabled… The post GitHub Copilot code review for Azure Repos is now in technical preview appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Shape Copilot code review around your team
item.description: Copilot code review adapts to your team’s tools and standards and scales its depth to the complexity of each change. Today we’re shipping two public previews on a unified agent… The post Shape Copilot code review around your team appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: A Developer’s Guide to Managing Models, Cost and Quality in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Learn a practical model lifecycle for Microsoft Foundry: select the right model, evaluate quality, optimize cost, operate safely, and improve as production needs change. The post A Developer’s Guide to Managing Models, Cost and Quality in Microsoft Foundry appeared first on Microsoft Foundry Blog.
item.dc:creator: Naomi Moneypenny

item.title: Extend GitHub with agent apps
item.description: Agent apps are AI agents from GitHub partners, installable from the GitHub Marketplace, and integrated directly into GitHub. You install an agent app just like any other GitHub App from… The post Extend GitHub with agent apps appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Microsoft Build 2026: Securing code, agents, and models across the development lifecycle
item.description: Discover how Microsoft enables fast, secure AI development with MDASH and new security capabilities. The post Microsoft Build 2026: Securing code, agents, and models across the development lifecycle appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Aleš Holeček

item.title: Start Secure, Stay Secure: How Microsoft is Closing the Gap from Code to Runtime
item.description: Modern software applications composed of hundreds of artifacts, multiple programing languages, and cloud infrastructure provide an extensive attack surface that only continues to expand in the era of AI. According to Microsoft Threat Intelligence and industry research, threat actors are also...
item.dc:creator: JasonWeber

item.title: Improved data processing features in Foundry IQ: Richer content extraction and data enrichment
item.description: Great answers start before retrieval. They depend on how well your data pipeline can reach enterprise content, preserve document structure, and carry text and visual context into the index. As part of the Microsoft Foundry platform, the Foundry IQ (Azure AI Search) 2026-05-01-preview release...
item.dc:creator: gia_mondragon

item.title: Foundry IQ: Improve recall by up to 54% with knowledge bases
item.description: By Alec Berntson, Alina Stoica Beck, Jim Singh, Amaia Salvador Aguilera, Lihang Li, Matt Gotteiner Agents are only valuable for the enterprise when they have access to your organization's knowledge. A common starting point is to give your agent a retrieval tool to find relevant context for its...
item.dc:creator: MattGotteiner

item.title: Foundry IQ: New governance and enterprise AI security capabilities
item.description: Enterprise AI adoption depends on more than retrieval quality. It requires a foundation that enables agents to access business-critical content without weakening the controls already in place to protect it, including source permissions, sensitivity labels, audit trails, private network paths, and...
item.dc:creator: gia_mondragon

item.title: What’s Coming Next in Visual Studio: Our Microsoft Build 2026 Announcements
item.description: Microsoft Build kicks off today in San Francisco, June 2 and 3. If you cannot make it in person, the sessions are streaming online for free, and I want to walk you through what we are announcing for Visual Studio this week. One idea tie most of it together. Code is an asset, not just […] The post...
item.dc:creator: Mads Kristensen

item.title: Build and run agents at scale with Microsoft Foundry at Build 2026
item.description: Learn how Microsoft Foundry helps developers build, deploy, and operate production-ready agents with Agent Framework, Toolboxes, hosted agents, Microsoft 365 distribution, observability, and agent optimization. The post Build and run agents at scale with Microsoft Foundry at Build 2026 appeared...
item.dc:creator: Tina Schuchman

item.title: Build agents you can trust across any framework with open evals and a control standard
item.description: Learn how Microsoft helps developers build trustworthy AI agents with open evaluations, portable runtime controls, production observability, and security workflows that work across frameworks. The post Build agents you can trust across any framework with open evals and a control standard appeared...
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bird

item.title: Foundry IQ: Build smarter agents faster with unified knowledge and serverless retrieval
item.description: Learn how Foundry IQ helps developers ground agents with unified enterprise knowledge, serverless retrieval, improved agentic retrieval quality, and production-ready security. The post Foundry IQ: Build smarter agents faster with unified knowledge and serverless retrieval appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Pablo Castro

item.title: Shaping what Azure SRE Agent does: Tool Permissions and Hooks
item.description: When an AI agent runs against production, the first question every security team asks is "What can it do, who decided it could, and what stops it from doing something it should not." Azure SRE Agent reached general availability in March. Since then, teams inside Microsoft and customers running it...
item.dc:creator: Dalibor_Kovacevic

item.title: Announcing Intelligent Terminal 0.1
item.description: Today we’re announcing Intelligent Terminal version 0.1, an open-source experimental fork of Windows Terminal with native agent integration. Get started Install from the Microsoft Store: Intelligent Terminal on the Microsoft Store Install via WinGet: winget install Microsoft.IntelligentTerminal...
item.dc:creator: Hamza Usmani

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | Build Edition
item.description: Microsoft Build 2026 brings a major set of Microsoft Foundry updates for developers building agents: hosted runtimes, Toolboxes, memory, Voice Live, Foundry IQ, new models, managed compute, and trust, evaluation, and observability tools. The post What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | Build Edition...
item.dc:creator: Nick Brady

item.title: Introducing Copilot CLI and agentic capabilities enhancements in JetBrains IDEs
item.description: With Copilot CLI now available in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs, this update centers on new capabilities for Copilot CLI sessions, while also delivering a broader set of agentic improvements.… The post Introducing Copilot CLI and agentic capabilities enhancements in JetBrains IDEs appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Gemini models in Copilot CLI, cloud agent, and the Copilot app
item.description: Two Gemini models are now available across additional GitHub Copilot surfaces. Gemini 3.1 Pro (Preview) and Gemini 3.5 Flash can now be used in: Copilot CLI Copilot cloud agent GitHub… The post Gemini models in Copilot CLI, cloud agent, and the Copilot app appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Copilot in Eclipse: BYOK, skills, and chat updates
item.description: This release of GitHub Copilot in Eclipse packs a lot: a refreshed chat experience, deeper visibility into your session context, BYOK for Business and Enterprise, better ABAP support, and brand… The post GitHub Copilot in Eclipse: BYOK, skills, and chat updates appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Building AI apps and agents with Azure Databricks, Copilot Studio, and GitHub Copilot
item.description: A workspace-wide Genie MCP endpoint for Copilot Studio Genie is Azure Databricks’ AI agent that lets any employee chat with their data and get trusted answers instantly. Genie Spaces are curated, business‑domain workspaces for teams to find strategic insights for their targeted use cases. Until...
item.dc:creator: Jason_Pereira

item.title: What’s New in Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description: A few weeks ago, we announced the public preview refresh of hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service — a fundamentally re-imagined agent runtime built for operationalizing production-grade AI agents in enterprise systems. Today at Microsoft Build, we are excited to share several updates that make...
item.dc:creator: Tina Manghnani, Pranav Pandit

item.title: MSSQL Extension for VS Code: Azure SQL Database Provisioning and More
item.description: The MSSQL extension for VS Code v1.43 expands what’s possible inside Visual Studio Code with the General Availability of Schema Designer with GitHub Copilot, Data API builder, and SQL Notebooks. We’re also introducing the Public Preview of Azure SQL Database provisioning, giving developers a guided...
item.dc:creator: Yo-Lei Chen, Carlos Robles, Tauseef Siddique

item.title: Azure SRE Agent at Microsoft Build 2026: Bringing agentic operations to the enterprise
item.description: Build 2026 Update When we launched Azure SRE Agent, the promise was simple: reduce operational toil, improve up time, and evolve teams from manual incident response towards AI-powered operations. Since GA in March 2026, that promise has held up in production. Teams are using the agent to diagnose...
item.dc:creator: dchelupati

item.title: Bring Your Own GitHub App: Connecting Azure SRE Agent to Enterprise Repositories
item.description: What if your SRE agent could access your enterprise GitHub repositories the same way your CI/CD pipelines do with a governed service identity, not a personal token? Azure SRE Agent connects to your GitHub repositories to build rich context about your systems source code, infrastructure definitions,...
item.dc:creator: dchelupati

item.title: Private Plugins with Azure SRE Agent
item.description: SRE's and platform teams are building operational skills specific to their infrastructure: investigation runbooks, compliance checks, cost analysis playbooks, deployment verification procedures. The next step is making that work reusable across every agent in the organization without exposing it...
item.dc:creator: ebencarek

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Snapshot backup for SQL Server in Azure VMs
item.description: Azure Backup now supports snapshot-based backup for SQL Server running on Azure Virtual Machines. This new capability uses Azure disk snapshots combined with native SQL transaction log backups to deliver near-instant, low-impact full backups for large SQL
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: How ACR Runs Multi-Tenancy at Scale: Compute Stamp Rebalancing and Why You Never See It Happen
item.description: By Johnson Shi, Richard Yuan, Yi Zha, Susan Shi, Jeanine Burke, Bin Du, Clark Porter, Bernie Harris, Eric Du Introduction Two of the most common questions we hear from teams running container workloads at scale on Azure Container Registry (ACR) are: "How does ACR keep my registry's performance...
item.dc:creator: johnsonshi_msft

item.title: Learning Agent now generally available — personalized AI upskilling for every employee
item.description: Meet Learning Agent in Microsoft 365 Copilot Today we’re excited to announce the general availability of Learning Agent in Microsoft 365 Copilot, to help every employee build Copilot and AI skills where the work gets done. It provides personalized recommendations based on each person’s context –...
item.dc:creator: Dan Zarzar

item.title: Monitor AI coding agents with OpenTelemetry in Azure Monitor
item.description: AI coding agents are quickly becoming part of the everyday developer workflow. As teams adopt tools such as GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, and Codex, they need a better way to understand usage, troubleshoot performance, and keep an eye on token consumption and cost. With Azure Monitor’s OpenTelemetry...
item.dc:creator: KayodePrince

item.title: 🔥Designing Specialized Agents - Model Selection, Tool Scoping, and Output Contracts🔥
item.description: aka, here's your toolbox and marching orders, bot
item.dc:creator: Kyler Middleton

item.title: Direct OpenTelemetry ingestion into Azure Monitor is now generally available
item.description: OpenTelemetry is powering a new era of observability. Built on open standards, designed for portability, and made for developers who want flexibility without compromise. And now, you can send OpenTelemetry logs, metrics, and traces straight into Azure Monitor OTLP endpoints and data storage. This...
item.dc:creator: KayodePrince

item.title: Infrastructure as Code for AI: Building and Deploying Microsoft Hosted Agents with Terraform
item.description: AI agents are no longer experimental. Teams are shipping production-grade agents that retrieve information, call APIs, reason over documents, and orchestrate multi-step workflows at scale. Microsoft Foundry's Hosted Agents service gives you a fully managed runtime for those agents, built on top of...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Retirement: Azure VMware Solution AV36 node End of Support on September 30, 2027
item.description: Reminder: Microsoft’s support lifecycle is evolving in alignment with VMware’s roadmap. Broadcom will end support for the current AV36 VCF version on September 30, 2027, and the AV36 SKU is not compatible with VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) 9, the next-gen
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Building and Operating a Microsoft Foundry Hosted Agent with GitOps and GitHub Tasks
item.description: The Gap Between Prototype and Production Most AI engineering teams can build a working agent in a day. The hard part is not building it; the hard part is operating it. Prompts drift. Tool configurations change without review. Deployments happen from someone's laptop. There is no audit trail, no...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Better Deployment Errors in az webapp deploy
item.description: Deployment failures can be difficult to interpret, especially when the error returned by the deployment API does not clearly explain what went wrong or what to do next. To make this easier, we have added a new switch to az webapp deploy for App Service for Linux: --enriched-errors true When...
item.dc:creator: TulikaC

item.title: Understand What’s Happening with Your App Service for Linux Website Using Site Status
item.description: When your website is not starting or behaving unexpectedly, it can be difficult to quickly understand what state the application is in and what might be causing the issue. To make this easier, Azure App Service for Linux now includes Site Status. Site Status provides runtime information for your...
item.dc:creator: TulikaC

item.title: Scaling multi-node LLM inference with NVIDIA Dynamo-Grove on AKS (Part 4)
item.description: Learn how NVIDIA Grove scheduler works with Dynamo to schedule, start, and scale inference deployments to match the underlying inference architecture.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Determinism over magic: the engineering design behind Azure Container Registry Regional Endpoints
item.description: By Zoey Li, Huangli Wu, Johnson Shi, Wei Meng Introduction Azure Container Registry (ACR) supports geo-replication: one registry resource with active-active replicas across multiple Azure regions. You push or pull through any replica, and ACR asynchronously replicates content to all others. For...
item.dc:creator: zoeyli

item.title: KT accelerates cloud and AI adoption with Azure Confidential Computing
item.description: A leading South Korea telecom provider, KT Corporation, serves more than 20 million consumers and thousands of enterprise customers in finance, manufacturing, and the public sector. As businesses across the country undergo digital transformation, KT needed to expand its cloud offerings but faced...
item.dc:creator: Rakeshginjupalli

item.title: Building a GitHub Copilot Agent Usage Dashboard
item.description: Introduction Working with organisations that are attempting to create GitHub Copilot custom agents, take-up of these agents by their community becomes important to know. Some questions quickly emerge are "how well are we actually using it?", "which agents are getting used and which have not had...
item.dc:creator: jometzg
